expectations.text: States isometrics can work for strength maintenance
expectations.evidence: States: 'isometric contractions can preserve 80-90% of strength levels over 4-8 weeks.'

expectations.text: Notes high-intent ballistic maximal contractions are most effective style
expectations.evidence: States holds must be at '80%+ of maximum voluntary contraction (MVC)' — near-maximal but doesn't specifically say ballistic/high-intent.

expectations.text: Explains strong angle specificity (~15-30 degrees transfer range)
expectations.evidence: States: 'angle specificity. Isometric strength gains are largely confined to the trained joint angle (typically ±15°).'
Gives setup suggestions: doorway frame pressing, heavy objects for deadlift holds, chair for squat holds.

expectations.text: Notes different fatigue profile due to lack of eccentric loading
expectations.evidence: States: 'Isometric training cannot: Build muscle mass' and mentions minimal hypertrophy — implies different fatigue profile.

expectations.text: Cautions that programming prescriptions are less standardized
expectations.evidence: Gives recommendations but notes 'evidence isn't definitive' for durations. States less clear than dynamic training.
